steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The researchers' conclusion is that Poland reported the greatest number of damaging insect species. The correct choice must cite Poland's insect count and show it exceeds both Italy's and Finland's insect counts — a direct three-country comparison using only the Insects column.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Italy reported 57 damaging fungus species and 42 damaging insect species, while both Poland and Finland reported more insect species than fungus species.: This mixes fungi and insects and highlights Italy, not Poland's insect supremacy. It does not support a conclusion about Poland having the most insect species. ✗
  • Choice B — Poland reported 105 damaging insect species, which is more than either Italy or Finland reported.: Poland's 105 insects directly exceeds Italy's 42 and Finland's 28 — exactly the data needed to support the conclusion. ✓
  • Choice C — Italy reported 57 damaging fungus species, which is more than either Poland or Finland reported.: This is about fungi, not insects, and features Italy rather than Poland. It supports a different conclusion entirely. ✗
  • Choice D — Italy and Finland reported 14 and 6 damaging insect species, respectively, which is far fewer than Poland reported.: The numbers 14 and 6 are tree counts, not insect species counts. This misreads the table and therefore cannot validly support the conclusion. ✗
That matches Choice B — Poland reported 105 damaging insect species, which is more than either Italy or Finland reported..
